Computer Vision Fundamentals.aiRubric: Check OpenCV basics, color spaces, filtering, thresholding, and Haar cascade face detection.

lessons.title: Image Processing Basics with OpenCV (Read, Display, Save)
lessons.theory:
## OpenCV Basics
OpenCV is the standard library for computer vision in Python.
```python
import cv2
img = cv2.imread('image.jpg')
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Read Image
Use `cv2` to read `'photo.png'` into a variable called `img`.

lessons.title: Color Spaces & Image Transformations
lessons.theory:
## Color Spaces
OpenCV reads images in BGR format by default. Often we convert to Grayscale or RGB.
```python
gray = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Convert to Grayscale
Convert the BGR image `img` to grayscale using `cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY`.

lessons.title: Image Filtering, Blurring & Edge Detection
lessons.theory:
## Edge Detection
Canny edge detection finds the edges in an image.
```python
edges = cv2.Canny(gray, 100, 200)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Canny Edges
Apply Canny edge detection to `gray_img` with thresholds `50` and `150`.

lessons.title: Thresholding & Contour Detection
lessons.theory:
## Thresholding
Converts a grayscale image into a binary (black and white) image.
```python
ret, thresh = cv2.threshold(gray, 127, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Binary Threshold
Apply a binary threshold to `gray` at a value of `100`.
lessons.starterCode:
import cv2
ret, thresh = cv2.___(___, ___, 255, cv2.___)
lessons.solution:
import cv2
ret, thresh = cv2.threshold(gray, 100, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

lessons.title: Face Detection with Haar Cascades
lessons.theory:
## Haar Cascades
Pre-trained classifiers used for rapid object detection (like faces).
```python
cascade = cv2.CascadeClassifier('haarcascade_frontalface_default.xml')
faces = cascade.detectMultiScale(gray, 1.1, 4)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Detect Faces
Use `detectMultiScale` on the `gray` image with a scale factor of `1.2` and `5` minNeighbors.

lessons.title: Core Concepts of Computer Vision Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Understanding Computer Vision Fundamentals\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of Computer Vision Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'Computer Vision Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.solution: def get_concept():\n    return 'Computer Vision Fundamentals Foundation'
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'Computer Vision Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for Computer Vision Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering Computer Vision Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to Computer Vision Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in Computer Vision Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying Computer Vision Fundamentals at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure Computer Vision Fundamentals projects.

lessons.solution: class Module:\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.name = 'Computer Vision Fundamentals'

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in Computer Vision Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in Computer Vision Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.title: Advanced Debugging in Computer Vision Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling Computer Vision Fundamentals Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management

Deep Learning for Vision.aiRubric: Check CNN concepts, PyTorch neural network logic, Transfer Learning, YOLO basics, and segmentation concepts.

lessons.title: Intro to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
lessons.theory:
## CNNs
CNNs use convolutional layers to extract spatial features from images.
```python
import torch.nn as nn
conv = nn.Conv2d(in_channels=3, out_channels=16, kernel_size=3)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Define Convolution
Create a 2D convolution layer with `1` input channel, `32` output channels, and a kernel size of `5`.
lessons.starterCode:
import torch.nn as nn
conv1 = nn.___(___=1, ___=32, ___=5)
lessons.solution:
import torch.nn as nn
conv1 = nn.Conv2d(in_channels=1, out_channels=32, kernel_size=5)

lessons.title: Building & Training a CNN with PyTorch
lessons.theory:
## PyTorch Training
A training step requires a forward pass, loss calculation, backpropagation, and optimizer step.
```python
loss.backward()
optimizer.step()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Backprop Step
Call the backward pass on the `loss` variable, then step the `optimizer`.
lessons.rubric: Backward pass and optimizer step completed.

lessons.title: Transfer Learning & Pre-Trained Models (ResNet)
lessons.theory:
## Transfer Learning
Use a pre-trained model and replace its final layer for a new task.
```python
import torchvision.models as models
resnet = models.resnet18(pretrained=True)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Load ResNet
Load a pre-trained `resnet50` model from `torchvision.models`.
lessons.starterCode:
import torchvision.models as models
model = models.___(___)
lessons.solution:
import torchvision.models as models
model = models.resnet50(pretrained=True)

lessons.title: Object Detection Basics with YOLO
lessons.theory:
## YOLO
You Only Look Once (YOLO) is a fast, single-pass object detection model predicting bounding boxes and classes.
```python
# pseudo-code: model(image) -> [x, y, w, h, class_id, confidence]
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: YOLO Output
Assign the string `'Bounding Box'` to the variable representing the spatial localization output of YOLO.

lessons.title: Image Segmentation Fundamentals
lessons.theory:
## Segmentation
Unlike detection (boxes), segmentation predicts a class for every single pixel in the image.
```python
# Output shape: [batch_size, num_classes, height, width]
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Segmentation Target
Assign the string `'Pixel'` to the variable describing what level segmentation operates at.

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Deep Learning for Vision.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Deep Learning for Vision.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Deep Learning for Vision.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

Advanced Vision & Deployment.aiRubric: Check object tracking concepts, facial recognition, VLM knowledge, ONNX export, and vision deployment logic.

lessons.title: Real-Time Object Tracking in Video
lessons.theory:
## Object Tracking
Algorithms like SORT or DeepSORT track an object ID across multiple video frames.
```python
tracker.update(detections)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Tracker Update
Call the `update` method on the `tracker` object, passing in the `boxes` array.
lessons.rubric: Tracker update method called with boxes.

lessons.title: Building a Facial Recognition Pipeline
lessons.theory:
## Facial Recognition
Extract face embeddings (vectors) and compare their cosine similarity or Euclidean distance.
```python
distance = np.linalg.norm(emb1 - emb2)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Vector Distance
Assign the string `'Euclidean'` to the variable describing the distance metric `np.linalg.norm` computes.

lessons.title: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) & Multimodal AI
lessons.theory:
## VLMs
Models like CLIP embed both text and images into the same vector space to perform zero-shot classification.
```python
image_features = model.encode_image(image)
text_features = model.encode_text(text)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Joint Embedding Space
Assign `'CLIP'` to the popular OpenAI VLM model that maps images and text together.

lessons.title: Optimizing Models with ONNX & TensorRT
lessons.theory:
## ONNX
Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) format allows cross-platform optimization.
```python
torch.onnx.export(model, dummy_input, "model.onnx")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Export to ONNX
Use `torch.onnx.export` to save `model` and `x` to `'vision.onnx'`.

lessons.title: Deploying Vision Models to Production
lessons.theory:
## Deployment
Vision models are heavy. We often use TensorRT or TorchServe for optimized production inference.
```bash
torchserve --start --ncs --model-store model_store --models resnet=resnet-18.mar
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: TorchServe
Assign `'TorchServe'` to the PyTorch tool used specifically for model serving.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in Advanced Vision & Deployment

lessons.title: API Integration Strategies

lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.
